agents.name: Rex
agents.note: Session 131 -- US-259 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 21 7/10 -- drain-test e2e harness expansion + lifecycle gate).  Goal: close the test-fidelity gap proven by drain test 5 (Sprint-20 US-245 harness PASSED yet production hard-crashed).  Root cause: US-245 was written before US-252 shipped, so its orchestrator construction predates the powerLogWriter parameter -- the harness never exercised the production stage->power_log forensic path.  TWO test changes shipped: (1) UPDATED tests/pi/integration/test_power_mgmt_e2e_drain.py -- threaded the lifecycle._createPowerLogWriter closure (logShutdownStage(db, eventType, vcell)) into all 3 orchestrator constructions + added _readStageRows + _buildPowerLogWriter helpers + assertions on stage_warning/stage_imminent/stage_trigger rows in power_log with per-stage VCELL boundary pinning (3.60/3.50/3.42 V for the canonical 4.20->3.42 trace).  Negative cases preserved: TestE2eRecoveryCancelsDrain asserts only WARNING stage row landed (IMMINENT/TRIGGER never fired because VCELL recovered); TestE2eSocPinnedDoesNotPreventTrigger asserts all 3 stage rows landed despite SOC pinned 60%, proving the post-US-234 VCELL ladder is the only path that could have produced them.  (2) NEW tests/pi/integration/test_power_mgmt_lifecycle.py -- 1 test / 1 class / ~370 lines.  Two-instance shape exercises the service-restart boundary the single-trace harness cannot: instance 1 walks startup -> 3-tick idle EXTERNAL (no transitions, no stage rows) -> wall-out + 7-tick drain to TRIGGER -> systemctl poweroff invoked -> drain_event row CLOSED before _shutdownAction fires; references dropped (synthetic poweroff boundary); instance 2 instantiates fresh UpsMonitor + BatteryHealthRecorder + PowerDownOrchestrator on the SAME persistent ObdDatabase; asserts (a) prior drain_event row's end_timestamp survives intact, (b) fresh orchestrator at NORMAL (no stale state across reboot), (c) 3-tick post-boot idle EXTERNAL fires ZERO new stage rows.  Mocks at I2cClient.readWord + subprocess.run boundaries per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md.  Discriminator (documented in BOTH test docstrings): files FAIL against pre-US-252 code -- if the powerLogWriter kwarg is removed the orchestrator's _writePowerLogStage no-ops AND the stage-row assertions cannot pass.  Verification: pytest tests/pi/integration/test_power_mgmt_e2e_drain.py tests/pi/integration/test_power_mgmt_lifecycle.py tests/pi/power/test_staged_shutdown_actually_fires.py -v -- 10/10 PASS in 50.06s (lifecycle test 0.10s call time -- pytest collection dominates).  Focused regression pytest tests/pi/power/ tests/pi/hardware/ tests/pi/integration/ -m 'not slow' -q -- 248 passed / 11 skipped / 0 failures in 116.30s.  Full fast suite pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' -- 3711 passed / 26 failed / 17 skipped in 14:43.  All 26 failures pre-existing on this branch: 24 TD-042 (theme stub drift, filed Session 121), 1 TD-044 (migration 0005 broken by v0006, filed pre-Sprint-21), 1 scripts/seed_eclipse_vin.py B-044 hardcoded-addresses lint (commit 35e1374 already on main).  ruff check clean on both touched test files.  sprint_lint 0 errors / 21 informational sizing-cap warnings.  GOTCHAS recorded: (a) the canonical drain trace [4.20, 4.10, 3.90, 3.75, 3.60, 3.50, 3.46, 3.42] hits stages at 3.60V (WARNING), 3.50V (IMMINENT, NOT 3.55V because IMMINENT only checks after WARNING entered same tick which falls through 3.55V <= currentVcell at 3.60V to False), 3.42V (TRIGGER, NOT 3.45V because 3.46V > 3.45V).  Pin per-stage values explicitly; assertions on threshold values themselves would mask a regression where a stage fired at the wrong threshold.  (b) Test runtime invariant '<10s' is call time not collection time -- pytest startup dominates ~30-50s for these integration files; actual orchestrator/database work is <1s.  --durations=10 confirms call=0.10s for lifecycle test.  (c) UpsMonitor.recordHistorySample is the seam to drive deterministic source-detection over a fake clock; the real UpsMonitor polling thread is NOT started in synthetic tests -- ticks are driven manually so timing is exact.  (d) Two-instance pattern across the simulated poweroff boundary: drop instance-1 references with `del orch1, recorder1, ups1`; instantiate fresh objects on the same lifecycleDb fixture; the DB persistence is what the test pins.
